objective.what: A high-performance, ECS-driven Private LTE/5G Core Network.
objective.why: To bypass legacy, slow-moving telecom stacks with a data-oriented, game-engine-inspired architecture optimized for low-latency routing and satellite handovers.
objective.how: Utilizing Rust for memory safety, ECS for state management, and eBPF/XDP for kernel-level packet processing.

tech_stack.math_engine: mid-math (Custom SIMD-optimized crate from Mid Engine)

architecture_structure.crates/midn-auth: Subscriber authentication — Milenage/TUAK algorithms, AKA procedure, Ki/OPc handling
architecture_structure.crates/midn-proto: Protocol stack — NAS, S1AP, NGAP, GTP-U zero-copy parser
architecture_structure.crates/midn-core: Control plane — MME/AMF state machine and ECS subscriber registry
architecture_structure.crates/midn-userplane: User plane — UPF routing, GTP tunnel management, eBPF loader
architecture_structure.crates/midn-userplane-ebpf: Kernel-space XDP program — packet steering at NIC speed
architecture_structure.crates/mid-math: External dependency from Mid Engine — signal geometry, handover math

phase_1.name: Foundation & Identity
phase_1.deliverable: Milenage AKA procedure producing (RES, CK, IK, AUTN) tuples

phase_2.name: Protocol Stack
phase_2.deliverable: Full LTE attach procedure simulated end-to-end

phase_3.name: User Plane
phase_3.deliverable: Packets routed at XDP speed through GTP-U tunnels
